2606.24960.title: Enhancing Clinician Decision-Making via Uncertainty-Aware Multi-Expert Fusion for Stroke Rehabilitation
2606.24960.paper_summary: Tailoring stroke rehabilitation requires assessing how movements are organized, not merely if they succeed. Currently, this assessment is a rate-limiting bottleneck. Instruments like the Action Research Arm Test (ARAT) compress rich behavioral observations into single ordinal endpoints, discarding the movement-quality details that distinguish recovery from compensation. Automated alternatives typically chase accuracy on noisy, single-observer labels to output opaque scores - a technology-centric approach that rarely reaches clinical practice. To address this, we present xAARA: an engine designed to augment rather than replace clinical judgment. From multi-view video, xAARA returns ARAT assessments with calibrated uncertainty and explanations across task, movement-phase, and movement-quality levels. Treating clinical scoring as an ill-posed inference problem, xAARA composes 692 calibrated multimodal models via a Dynamic Bayesian Network with entropy-based gating. It qualifies results against clinical validity rules and defers low-confidence cases. In 105 stroke survivors (788 exercises), xAARA achieved 94.2% task accuracy (Cohen's kappa=0.934) and 81.3% movement-phase accuracy (kappa=0.727), reducing predictive uncertainty by 96.1% compared to single-clinician scoring. For subjective cases, it matched at least one rater 100% of the time and never returned out-of-range scores. Four independent clinicians validated the assessments and indicated willingness to adopt the system. We argue that principled uncertainty quantification and clinician-aligned explainability are the critical bridges moving automated assessment from technical demonstration to a deployable clinical tool.
2606.24960.paper_summary_zh: 量身定制的中風康復需要評估動作的組織方式，而不僅僅是評估它們是否成功。<lb/>目前，這種評估是一個限制性瓶頸。<lb/>像行動研究手臂測試（ARAT）這樣的工具將豐富的行為觀察壓縮為單一的序數端點，捨棄了區分恢復與補償的運動質量細節。<lb/>自動化的替代方案通常追求在嘈雜的單一觀察者標籤上獲得準確性，以輸出不透明的分數——這是一種以技術為中心的方法，鮮少進入臨床實踐。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了xAARA：一個旨在增強而非取代臨床判斷的引擎。<lb/>從多視角視頻中，xAARA返回帶有校準不確定性和解釋的ARAT評估，涵蓋任務、運動階段和運動質量等級別。<lb/>將臨床評分視為一個不良定義的推斷問題，xAARA通過一個基於熵的動態貝葉斯網絡組合692個校準的多模態模型。<lb/>它根據臨床有效性規則對結果進行質量檢查，並推遲低信心的案例。<lb/>在105名中風倖存者（788次練習）中，xAARA達到了94.2%的任務準確率（Cohen's kappa=0.934）和81.3%的運動階段準確率（kappa=0.727），與單一臨床醫生評分相比，預測不確定性降低了96.1%。<lb/>對於主觀案例，它100%與至少一位評分者匹配，並且從未返回超出範圍的分數。<lb/>四位獨立臨床醫生驗證了這些評估並表示願意採用該系統。<lb/>我們認為，原則性的不確定性量化和與臨床醫生對齊的可解釋性是將自動化評估從技術演示轉變為可部署臨床工具的關鍵橋樑。
2606.24960.author: Tamim Ahmed et.al.
